nodes.name: 新南威爾士州
nodes.summary: 新南威爾士州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、叢塚雉、Black-eared cuckoo等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 南澳大利亚州
nodes.summary: 南澳大利亚州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括Dusky moorhen、鹗、黄尾黑凤头鹦鹉等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 外来捕食者
nodes.summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。

nodes.name: Granite Belt
nodes.summary: Granite Belt是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Chestnut-rumped heathwren，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Chestnut-rumped heathwren
nodes.summary: Chestnut-rumped heathwren（Chestnut-rumped heathwren，学名 Hylacola pyrrhopygia），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括外来捕食者。

nodes.name: 維多利亞州
nodes.summary: 維多利亞州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联29种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、Black-eared cuckoo、巨地鸠等29种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The chestnut-rumped heathwren occurs in southeastern Australia from the Granite Belt of southeast Queensland through eastern New South Wales, Victoria, and southeast South Australia.Morcombe, Michael (2012) Field Guide to Australian Birds

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The chestnut-rumped heathwren occurs in southeastern Australia from the Granite Belt of southeast Queensland through eastern New South Wales, Victoria, and southeast South Australia.Morcombe, Michael (2012) Field Guide to Australian Birds

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The chestnut-rumped heathwren occurs in southeastern Australia from the Granite Belt of southeast Queensland through eastern New South Wales, Victoria, and southeast South Australia.Morcombe, Michael (2012) Field Guide to Australian Birds

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The chestnut-rumped heathwren occurs in southeastern Australia from the Granite Belt of southeast Queensland through eastern New South Wales, Victoria, and southeast South Australia.Morcombe, Michael (2012) Field Guide to Australian Birds

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: However, it is listed as Endangered in South Australia and Vulnerable in Victoria, with continued fragmentation and loss of habitat, and predation by introduced predators, being regarded as the main pressures
